skill.name: perigon-agent
skill.description: Guides agents to follow Perigon project conventions, source locations, and clarification rules before generating code.
skill.summary: Apply Perigon conventions before code changes.
skill.author: AterDev

security_audit.summary: The static hardcoded URL finding is a true positive because the skill references external Perigon documentation, but it is documentation guidance rather than data exfiltration. The weak cryptographic algorithm and system reconnaissance alerts are false positives from ordinary project guidance text. No prompt injection, command execution, secret access, or malicious intent was found in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: External Documentation Link
low_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: SKILL.md line 12 contains a hardcoded external documentation URL. This is a low-risk network trust concern, but it points to project documentation and does not send data externally.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL is directly visible on line 12. The surrounding text frames it as documentation, with no request to transmit secrets or local data.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm Static Match
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The static alert maps to the front matter description on line 3. No cryptographic algorithm, hashing routine, or encryption behavior appears there.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is a plain-language skill description. There is no code or security primitive to support the weak cryptography finding.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Static Match
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The line 13 alert is project architecture guidance about API, Entity Framework, and error-handling conventions. It does not instruct system probing or environment discovery.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The text is limited to development conventions. No commands, host inspection, credential search, or file enumeration instructions are present.

low_findings.title: Duplicate System Reconnaissance Static Match
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The duplicate line 13 alert refers to the same development convention text. No separate reconnaissance behavior is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: This finding duplicates the same safe line as the previous alert. The context remains ordinary framework and coding guidance.

low_findings.title: Clarification Guidance Static Match
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The line 14 alert tells the agent to ask for entity, DTO, module, or service details when unclear. This reduces unsafe assumptions and does not perform reconnaissance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 14 asks for user clarification and discourages automatic builds or migrations. It contains no instruction to inspect the system or collect sensitive data.

content.value_statement: Agents can miss project-specific rules before editing Perigon code. This skill points Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to key conventions, source locations, and clarification steps.
actual_capabilities: Directs agents to start with the repository copilot instructions before generating code.
Maps backend definitions, managers, DTOs, controllers, and host locations.
Maps Angular routes, services, shared components, and localization assets.
References Perigon documentation for development, data, API, and directory conventions.
Lists default patterns for REST APIs, ManagerBase, Code First EF, Guid v7 IDs, and error handling.
Encourages clarification before changing unclear entities, DTOs, modules, or services.
limitations: It does not contain full Perigon documentation content.
It depends on an external documentation URL for deeper guidance.
It does not validate code, run tests, or inspect diagnostics by itself.
It is tailored to repositories that follow the referenced Perigon structure.

use_cases.title: Prepare A Perigon Change
use_cases.description: Review expected source locations and coding defaults before editing a Perigon backend or Angular frontend.

use_cases.title: Align Agent Output
use_cases.description: Guide an AI assistant to follow project conventions before generating controllers, managers, DTOs, or frontend services.

use_cases.title: Onboard To Repository Layout
use_cases.description: Find the main backend and frontend directories when working in a Perigon-style repository for the first time.

prompt_templates.title: Find The Right Location
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the perigon-agent guidance to identify where this change belongs before editing files: [describe feature].
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner prompt for locating code areas.

prompt_templates.title: Plan A Backend Change
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply Perigon conventions and outline the files needed for a backend change involving [entity], [DTO], and [service].

prompt_templates.title: Review API Pattern Fit
prompt_templates.prompt: Check whether this proposed API design follows Perigon defaults for REST, ManagerBase, projections, and error handling: [paste design].

prompt_templates.title: Coordinate Full Stack Work
prompt_templates.prompt: Using Perigon backend and Angular maps, plan a full stack implementation for [feature]. Ask clarifying questions before assuming module details.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced prompt for multi-area implementation planning.

output_examples.input: Where should I add a new customer manager and DTO?
output_examples.output: The agent identifies the target module, then points to managers and models under the module structure. It asks for the module name if missing.

output_examples.input: Review my controller plan for a Perigon service.
output_examples.output: The agent checks RESTful API shape, controller placement, manager use, error handling, and whether projections avoid heavy includes.

output_examples.input: Plan the Angular side of a new feature.
output_examples.output: The agent maps likely route, service, shared component, and localization asset areas, then requests missing feature or module details.

best_practices: Read the repository copilot instructions before making code changes.
Ask for entity, DTO, module, and service details when they are unclear.
Use the external Perigon documentation only as supporting context.
anti_patterns: Do not assume target modules or services when the request is ambiguous.
Do not auto-run builds or migrations unless the user asks.
Do not wrap API responses when the project convention forbids ApiResponse wrappers.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps agents follow Perigon repository conventions before planning or generating code.

faq.question: Does it modify files automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance for agents and does not execute changes by itself.

faq.question: Why is there a network risk factor?
faq.answer: The skill links to external Perigon documentation. The link is a documentation reference, not a data transfer instruction.

faq.question: Can it replace the full documentation?
faq.answer: No. It summarizes key pointers and sends agents to the documentation for complete rules.

faq.question: Which tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: When should an agent ask questions?
faq.answer: The skill says to ask when entity, DTO, module, or service details are unclear.
